note: language-neutral: decode base64url → bytes → checkAuthorityProofBytes(package, config); assert result and (for a negative) that reason contains code.

vectors.note: genuine corroborated proof
quorum basis in aeq; support carries no quorum
pretty-printed (non-canonical) package bytes
duplicate JSON keys collapse on parse, fail round-trip
invalid UTF-8 package bytes
a JSON array is not a { term, witnesses } package
a constructor outside RULE_CONTRACTS
Corroborated with a 5th child
Corroborated with a 2nd witness
a coordinate param on QuorumAgreement (must derive from πChain)
a node carrying `expected`
two intervals on different clocks cannot be ordered
a padded (non-canonical) checkpoint_authority pub
a transparency-log receipt carrying pow-style {substrate,position} facts (not its log_id/index) — closed-facts violation (round-10 P0-03)
an interval with lower>upper yields no order
After orders unrelated evidences
checkpoint domain_shard ≠ genesis domain
C0 key-log length 257 > 256
votes for a foreign domain are not counted
attacker witnesses not admitted by config
a tampered witness fails its content address
witness pubs swapped at the same key_ids: quorum breaks + config_id changes
whitespace-prefixed (non-canonical) config bytes
an extra top-level package field (schema not closed over raw JSON)
an explicit params:{} node — a second wire form of the canonical (no-params) term
Anchored with required s/subject omitted
object-valued trust domains are rejected at config decode (ControlDomain is a typed string)
a receipt whose sig.alg is not Ed25519 (valid sig bytes)
a quorum vote with sig.alg not Ed25519 is not admitted
an unknown top-level config field
policy.uniqueness_threshold as a string is rejected, not silently defaulted
NameBound key-log witness that is an object, not an array
a receipt witness with an unknown outer field
a checkpoint witness with an unknown outer field
a terminality witness with an unknown field
a uniqueness attestation whose claim has an extra field is not admitted
a connector value that is a string, not an object
policy is an array, not an object
allowed_proof_kinds not sorted/de-duplicated
policy:[] (empty array) must not become a silent default threshold (round-10 P0-01)
a decomposed (non-NFC) trust_domain → stable E-CONFIG, not a throw (round-10 P1-02)
a checkpoint sig wrapper carrying an extra field (round-10 P0-04)
an authority-signed checkpoint body with an unknown field (round-10 P0-05)
a receipt claim missing issued_at, re-signed by the issuer (round-10 P0-02)
a content-addressed witness not referenced by the term (round-10 P1-03)
